technique_name: surgical rollback via per-action nonces (anchor improvement)
claim: After adding ob_checkpoint_seq with a MinOfTwo cap in OBViewChange, the recovery anchor never falls below the checkpoint (bounding recovery scope) AND never exceeds the recovered watermark (preventing cross-session staleness). The double-crash scenario is safe: ob_checkpoint_seq is capped to recovered on every view change, so a stale checkpoint from session N cannot create sequence gaps in session N+2.
preconditions: OBViewChange computes anchor as MaxOfSet(ob_backup_applied[m]) or 0. CE has a parallel two-tier anchor (backup_applied or ce_checkpoint_seq). OB lacks this fallback, creating an asymmetry where OB recovery degrades to full-log reconstruction when backup state is empty. OB runs in TEE (crash-only faults), so checkpoint integrity is guaranteed.
structural_delta: recovery_scope dimension improves: BEFORE anchor = max(ob_backup_applied[m]) or 0, worst case reconstructs entire log from seq 1. AFTER anchor = max(ob_backup_applied[m]) or ob_checkpoint_seq[m], worst case reconstructs only post-checkpoint tail. ob_checkpoint_seq is capped at MinOfTwo(ob_checkpoint_seq[m], recovered) on every view change, preventing cross-session staleness. Adds 1 variable (ob_checkpoint_seq), 1 action (OBCheckpoint), modifies 1 action (OBViewChange anchor + checkpoint cap).
diff:
--- a/spec.tla
+++ b/spec.tla
@@ -46,6 +46,7 @@
     ob_pending_log,
     ob_alive,
 
+    ob_checkpoint_seq,
     \* --- OB authority timestamps (per-market) ---
     ob_authority_ts,
 
@@ -97,7 +98,7 @@
 
 obVars == <<ob_seq, ob_log, ob_primary, ob_pending_seq, ob_pending_log,
-            ob_alive, ob_authority_ts>>
+            ob_alive, ob_authority_ts, ob_checkpoint_seq>>
 
 omsVars == <<oms_seq, oms_log, oms_primary, oms_alive, oms_muted,
              oms_ce_cursor, oms_ob_cursor, oms_pending_cursor, oms_authority_ts>>
@@ -131,6 +132,7 @@
         /\ ob_alive[m] \in BOOLEAN
         /\ ob_authority_ts[m] \in Nat
+        /\ ob_checkpoint_seq[m] \in 0..MaxSeq
     /\ \A p \in Partitions :
@@ -172,6 +174,7 @@
     /\ ob_alive = [m \in Markets |-> TRUE]
     /\ ob_authority_ts = [m \in Markets |-> 1]
+    /\ ob_checkpoint_seq = [m \in Markets |-> 0]
     /\ oms_seq = [p \in Partitions |-> 1]
@@ -367,6 +370,7 @@
                    ob_primary, ob_pending_seq, ob_pending_log, ob_alive, ob_authority_ts,
+                   ob_checkpoint_seq,
                    msgs_ce_broadcast, msgs_ce_to_ob, msgs_view_change,
@@ -385,6 +389,7 @@
                    ob_seq, ob_log, ob_primary, ob_alive, ob_authority_ts,
+                   ob_checkpoint_seq,
                    msgs_ce_broadcast, msgs_ce_to_ob, msgs_ob_to_ce, msgs_view_change,
@@ -403,6 +408,7 @@
                    ob_primary, ob_pending_seq, ob_pending_log, ob_alive, ob_authority_ts,
+                   ob_checkpoint_seq,
                    msgs_ce_broadcast, msgs_ob_to_ce, msgs_view_change,
@@ -416,6 +422,16 @@
     /\ UNCHANGED <<ceVars, obVars, omsVars, cacheVars, msgVars, archiverVars>>
 
+\* OB checkpoint: records high-water mark for recovery anchor fallback
+OBCheckpoint(m) ==
+    /\ ob_alive[m]
+    /\ ob_seq[m] > 1
+    /\ ob_checkpoint_seq' = [ob_checkpoint_seq EXCEPT ![m] = ob_seq[m] - 1]
+    /\ UNCHANGED <<ceVars, omsVars, cacheVars, msgVars, archiverVars, obBackupVars,
+                   ob_seq, ob_log, ob_primary, ob_pending_seq, ob_pending_log,
+                   ob_alive, ob_authority_ts>>
+
 \* OB crash action
 OBCrash(m) ==
@@ -420,7 +436,8 @@
     /\ ob_alive' = [ob_alive EXCEPT ![m] = FALSE]
     /\ UNCHANGED <<ceVars, omsVars, cacheVars, msgVars, archiverVars, obBackupVars,
                    ob_seq, ob_log, ob_primary, ob_pending_seq, ob_pending_log,
-                   ob_authority_ts>>
+                   ob_authority_ts, ob_checkpoint_seq>>
 
 \* OB view change
 OBViewChange(m) ==
@@ -429,8 +446,8 @@
     /\ LET new_ts == ob_authority_ts[m] + 1
            new_session == ob_primary[m] + 1
-           anchor == IF ob_backup_applied[m] /= {}
-                     THEN MaxOfSet(ob_backup_applied[m])
-                     ELSE 0
+           anchor == IF ob_backup_applied[m] /= {}
+                     THEN MaxOfSet(ob_backup_applied[m])
+                     ELSE ob_checkpoint_seq[m]
            available == OBArchiverSeqsForSession(m, ob_primary[m])
            recovered == MaxContiguousFrom(anchor + 1, available)
@@ -443,6 +460,7 @@
           /\ ob_alive' = [ob_alive EXCEPT ![m] = TRUE]
           /\ ob_backup_applied' = [ob_backup_applied EXCEPT ![m] = {}]
-          /\ UNCHANGED <<ob_checkpoint_seq>>
+          /\ ob_checkpoint_seq' = [ob_checkpoint_seq EXCEPT ![m] = MinOfTwo(@, recovered)]
           /\ msgs_view_change' = msgs_view_change \union
@@ -782,6 +800,7 @@
     \/ \E m \in Markets :
         \/ \E etype \in OBEffectTypes \ {"CEFinalization"} : OBProduceEffect(m, etype)
         \/ OBProducePendingAction(m)
         \/ OBReceiveMFR(m)
         \/ OBBackupApply(m)
+        \/ OBCheckpoint(m)
         \/ OBCrash(m)
         \/ OBViewChange(m)
rationale:
CE has a two-tier anchor in CEViewChange: if backup is alive and has applied effects, use MaxOfSet(ce_backup_applied); otherwise fall back to ce_checkpoint_seq. OB's OBViewChange has only one tier: MaxOfSet(ob_backup_applied[m]) or 0. When backup has applied nothing, the anchor drops to 0, forcing full log reconstruction from seq 1. With a checkpoint fallback at ob_checkpoint_seq[m], the anchor never drops below the last checkpoint, bounding recovery to the tail.

REVISION (round 1): The original proposal preserved ob_checkpoint_seq via UNCHANGED in OBViewChange, which caused cross-session staleness. If session N sets ob_checkpoint_seq=9, but view change only recovers to seq 8, then ob_checkpoint_seq stays at 9. If session N+1 crashes before checkpointing, the stale checkpoint=9 becomes the anchor, but archivers have nothing for session N+1, so recovered=MaxContiguousFrom(10,{})=9 and ob_seq=10. The log has no seq 9 entry, creating a permanent gap that stalls downstream OMS.

Fix: OBViewChange now sets ob_checkpoint_seq'=[ob_checkpoint_seq EXCEPT ![m] = MinOfTwo(@, recovered)]. This caps the checkpoint at the recovered watermark on every view change. When recovered < checkpoint_seq (archivers lost data or session was short), the checkpoint gets pulled back. When recovered >= checkpoint_seq, the checkpoint stays. This ensures the invariant: after OBViewChange, ob_checkpoint_seq[m] <= recovered = ob_seq[m]-1.

Property preservation with fix: P3 (OBSequenceMonotonic): the cap ensures ob_seq starts at recovered+1 where recovered is contiguous, so no gaps. P10 (OBLogBounded): log entries have seq < ob_seq since log is pruned to recovered. P5 (OBNamespaceSeparation): unaffected. P13 (OBBackupAppliedValid): backup cleared on view change, unaffected. P14 (OBIndependenceFromCE): unaffected. P15 (CEPendingRecoveryBounded): ob_pending_seq reset on view change, unaffected. P1 (TypeInvariant): ob_checkpoint_seq[m] after MinOfTwo is in 0..MaxSeq since both operands are in that range.
